LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER ANGOTTI & REILLY, INC.'S MOTION FOR COSTS OF ENFORCING JUDGMENT IS GRANTED IN PART. PETITIONER IS AWARDED FEES AND COSTS OF $241,370.00 INCURRED IN ENFORCING ITS JUDGMENT IN THIS ACTION. WHILE PETITIONER IS ENTITLED TO THE RECOVERY OF FEES AND PETITIONER HAS MADE A REASONABLE GOOD FAITH EFFORT TO SUPPORTS ITS REQUEST WITHOUT COMPROMISING ITS LITIGATION POSITIONS, ATTORNEY'S FEE IS REDUCED TO $240,000.00 WHICH IS APPROPRIATE DUE TO THE INSUFFICIENCY OF SOME OF THE INFORMATION PROVIDED BECAUSE OF BLOCK-BILLING AND REDACTIONS. Ntc Of Motion For Costs Of Enforcing Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-11-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PAUL W. BERNING, TEL. NO. (510) 863-8118 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. BLAKE CUNNINGHAM OF GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP, TEL. NO. 655-1318 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: PETITIONER ANGOTTI & REILLY, INC.'S Motion For Costs Of Enforcing Judgment is Granted in part. Petitioner is awarded fees and costs of $241,370.00 incurred in enforcing its judgment in this action. While petitioner is entitled to the recovery of fees and petitioner has made a reasonable good faith effort to supports its request without compromising its litigation positions, attorney's fee is reduced to $240,000.00 which is appropriate due to the insufficiency of some of the information provided because of block-billing and redactions. (See Order for details) ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/HEK)
